calendar.matters:
Petition Under Pc Sec. 12251 To Terminate Probate Proceedings Due To Lack Of Estate Assets And Discharge Of Personal Representative.
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-13-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eduardo Sandoval for Amalia Gonzalez.

Mr. Sandoval explained case history. The Court advised Mr. Sandoval that he was now asking the Court for something different than what was requested in the initial petition and that he needed to file a Petition to Termnate under Probate Code 12251. The Petition for Approval of First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. was taken off calendar.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status - Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-25-2012 Continued To Oct-17-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition
Off Calendar May-23-2011 Advanced From Jun-21-11 To Jun-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition
Off Calendar Jun-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John L. Adams for Amalia Gonzalez.

The Court stated that it received the declaration from Amparo's Daughter stating that she received the $70,000 check and inquired if Amparo had any other heirs. Mr. Adams stated that they weren't aware of any other heirs of Amparo, that they took a copy of the $70,000 check to Bank of America to ascertain whether Amparo Gonzalez had cashed it, and that the bank won't disclose that information without a court order. In addition, he stated that there is no opposition to the petition, that Ms. Prentice could not be here today but she did not object because there is no prejudice to her client's interest in the property, that Code of Civil Procedure 1060 allows the Court to proceed, that the 850 petition was dismissed back in January pursuant to a mediated settlement agreement, and that the reason they are asking for this declaration is because Ms. Gonzalez is willing to buy out the grandchildren of the decedent and in order for it to close escrow that they need to remove the cloud on the title. The Court then granted the petition declaring that Amparo Gonzalez is not an owner of the real property at 423 Faxon Avenue (subject to review/modification of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Application For Order Shortening Time And Order
Off Calendar May-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Determine Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Continued To Feb-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lois Prentice for Jessica Daniel and Alexander Vernon Daniel (Objectors). Eduardo Sandoval for Amalia Gonzalez.

Ms. Prentice stated that she filed a partition action in civil case number CGC-10-505451. Mr. Sandoval argued that it was proper to hear this matter in probate rather than with the civil action. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation (order for the same signed in open court). Mediation is to be completed by February 2, 2011. The Parties are to report status of mediation to the Probate Examiner by February 4, 2011. The matter was continued to February 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Aguirre.
